Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:32 ET
Institutional dispersion hedge builds as single-name calls surge amid index put protection
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion trades — index puts (1.33) hedge single-name call exposure (0.68).
  • 2. Negative SPY gamma at -$1.7B amplifies all directional moves while implied correlation hits 0.06, creating stock-picking regime.
  • 3. GDP data in 3 days threatens current positioning as vol divergences flag structural tension across sectors.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge building

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between indices and single names — index put/call ratios at 1.33 while single-name ratios hit 0.68, creating the widest spread in months. This structure screams institutional dispersion hedging, where funds load single-name calls while buying index puts as portfolio insurance, enabled by implied correlation collapsing to 0.06. The trade works when stock-specific moves dominate systematic risk, exactly what we're seeing with 12 names in backwardation signaling idiosyncratic event risk. Dealer positioning amplifies this dynamic with SPY gamma at -$1.7B, meaning any directional move gets magnified as dealers hedge short gamma positions. Combined with low correlation, this creates an explosive setup for individual names breaking from index performance. The vol surface confirms this tension — 5 names showing "vol high/skew flat" divergence while XLK exhibits "tech bull/skew fear," suggesting institutional hedging demand despite bullish positioning. Critical data risk emerges with GDP Q1 Advance in 3 days, threatening current positioning structures. UNH's massive two-way flow (+1269% calls, +1070% puts) ahead of potential healthcare policy implications, while the extreme spike names (NRIX +130,900% puts, ALLE +93,620% calls) suggest event-driven positioning accelerating into the macro release.

Layer 1: P/C divergence 1.33/0.68Layer 2: SPY GEX -$1.7BLayer 4: IC 0.06 stock-specific
UNHVOL PLAY

Massive two-way flow with calls +1269% and puts +1070% suggests positioning ahead of potential policy shifts. IV cheap at -20.3% vs sector despite elevated volume. Unusual for healthcare to see this vol demand outside earnings.

Institutional reversal story continues with Millennium adding 93% Q4 after puts-to-conviction flip. Block put flow at Apr 22 200P (114,078 contracts) against technical overbought (RSI 85) creates squeeze potential if momentum continues.

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
